attr_dc_description: The University of Bern is a member of Scholars at Risk (SAR), an international network of universities that aims to protect researchers who suffer grave threats to their lives, freedom and well-being. title: Institute of Plant Sciences
intro_text:
Understanding of how plants function, grow and develop, and complete their life cycles, has been of fundamental scientific interest for centuries. Today, basic research covers many scales that integrate physiology and ecology, from molecules to cells through to whole plants and vegetation. Important applications are to be found in areas such as agriculture, conservation and climate change.
The Institute of Plant Sciences carries out research in Molecular Plant Physiology, Chemical Ecology, Plant Ecology, and Palaeoecology. It contributes to the teaching programs in BSc Biology and to several MSc courses
The IPS has strong links to the Institute of Ecology and Evolution, the Institute of Cell Biology and the Wyss Academy for Nature.

titlealt: Unit for the Study of the Middle East and Muslim Societies
title: Unit for the Study of the Middle East and Muslim Societies
intro_lead: Ahlan wa-sahlan! Welcome to the Unit for the Study of the Middle East and Muslim Societies
intro_text:
Studying in Bern means studying at an excellent university. Studying Islamic and Middle Eastern Studies in Bern means studying in a departmental unit that not only deals with the ‘normative’ aspects of Islam, but also with the history, culture and everyday life in Muslim societies.

We focus primarily on modernity and the present while combining the disciplinary rigour of text-oriented Islamic Studies with the expertise of regional studies and postcolonial approaches. We are specialized in historical, sociological and political methods and work in particular on gender and sexuality studies, legal history, labour history, research on media and political movements.

As a smaller unit within the structure of a larger department, we offer excellent supervision and guidance for students pursuing their studies with us.

title: Clinical Anesthesiology
intro_text:
The Section of Anesthesia offers small animal, equine, ruminant and exotic anesthetics at the highest level. We are particularly interested in the pain therapy, which is also our main research area. Furthermore, we are also the persons to contact for veterinary practitioners, as well as other medical institutions working with exotic or laboratory animals, should they need professional help in managing difficult anesthesia cases or have basic questions on the planning of anesthesia protocols (Good Clinical Practice).
Our section is a recognized and successful training center for prospective veterinary anesthetists, accredited by the European College of Veterinary Anesthesia and Analgesia since 2000.

title: Institute of Old Catholic Theology
intro_lead: Institute of Old Catholic Theology
intro_text:
Old Catholic Theology has been taught and learned at the University of Bern for 150 years. It is:
- rooted in the Western Catholic tradition with its origins in the Eastern tradition
- ecumenically oriented
- focused on history and contemporary culture
- internationally networked
Our team reflects the essence of our institute. We speak nine different mother tongues and host various theological expertise.
The Institute of Old Catholic Theology offers unique opportunities for study and research in the international university landscape. We look forward to welcoming students interested in relevant and scientifically reflected theology. Studying theology qualifies students for work in the church, an academic career, or a career in the cultural humanities.

title: SuN-Conference 2026
intro_lead: Conference Sport and Sustainability, 8-10 April 2026
intro_text: Sustainability is also becoming an increasingly urgent issue in sport. Due to dynamically growing ecological and social challenges (e.g. climate change, social inequalities), sports organisations are increasingly con-fronted with questions regarding the sustainable and responsible design of their activities. When planning and implementing sports programmes, building sports facilities and staging events, there are many interactions and conflicting goals between the sustainability dimensions of the envi-ronment, society and the economy. This is precisely where we want to start with our conference on Sport and Sustainability. We are delighted that we have been able to bring together three sport science communities – the Sport Economics Working Group, the dvs-Section Sport Sociology and the Sportainable think tank – to work together on this topic. We are planning a diverse scientific programme that will use different for-mats to address on the one hand the question of how activities in the con-text of sport can be more closely aligned with the criteria of sustainable development and on the other hand how the potential of sport can be used even more effectively for overarching sustainability goals. The aim is not only to highlight knowledge bases and sustainability concepts, but above all to discuss ways and innovative approaches to sustainable action.
breadcrumbs: University of Bern > Portal > Microsites > SuN-Conference 2026

title: Institute of History
intro_lead: Welcome to the Institute of History at the University of Bern
intro_text:
We research and teach the study of history in all its thematic breadth and methodological diversity. Our research deals with the history of Europe from antiquity to the present, as well as with the history of regions outside of Europe.

Special focal points are the history of Latin America, Eastern European history as well as environmental and climate history. Our institute is the only one in German-speaking Switzerland with its own department of Swiss history, which deals with our country's past from the Middle Ages to the 21st century.
seometa_tag: The Institute of History offers study programs in the two-stage Bachelor-Master-System as well as a broad doctoral program

title: Faculty Clinical Research Platform (FCRP)
intro_text: The Faculty Clinical Research Platform (FCRP) fosters careers of young clinical researchers at the Vetsuisse Faculty of the University of Bern. To this end the FCRP launches competitive research funding calls for clinical research in the framework of PhD studies, habilitations, assistant lectureships and assistant professorships. Supported projects are intended to exploit synergies between clinical and non-clinical disciplines. The FCRP also provides guidance in career development and coordinates the availability of laboratory facilities and techniques for clinical researchers at the faculty. The overarching goal is to assist in the development of the next generations of academics in veterinary clinical sciences.

title: Osteology Research Network (ORN)
intro_lead: Welcome to the Osteology Research Network (ORN)
intro_text:
The Osteology Research Network (ORN) is a multidisciplinary collaboration that connects scientists, clinicians, and experts in the fields of bone and muscle biology. Our primary goal is to address the growing global health challenges posed by musculoskeletal diseases, including osteoporosis, sarcopenia, fractures, and other bone and muscle-related disorders.

Our network encourages innovative approaches that span basic research, translational studies, and clinical applications. Together, we aim to bridge the gap between laboratory findings and real-world treatments.

intro_lead: Advancing knowledge together for animals, people, and the planet
intro_text:
Our students should develop their analytical and conceptual skills, critical judgment, and sustainable concepts. 
How? We integrate innovative teaching concepts (electronic voting systems and internet-based quizzes) into blended learning, namely traditional lectures and digital case-based learning methods.

In our research, we are investigating new targets for therapy or prevention of neurodegenerative diseases such as Parkinson's disease. We are also investigating possible effects of non-ionizing radiation used in mobile communication technologies on health.

title: LIMS
intro_text:
Following the successful deployment of the Solar Wind Collector (SWC) instrument by the University of Bern – at that time the only non-US science team to participate in NASA’s first Apollo 11 mission to land on the lunar surface in 1969 – it is now its time for Bern’s second instrument: a sensitive Laser Ablation Laser Ablation Ionization Mass Spectrometer for the chemical composition analysis of lunar regolith at the lunar south pole.

Selected by NASA in 2021 under the Commercial Lunar Payload Services (CLPS) initiative, which is part of NASA's Artemis program, the Laser Ablation Ionization Mass Spectrometer (CLPS-LIMS), developed by the Space Research and Planetology Division of the Physics Institute at the University of Bern, Switzerland, will be part of the payload suite of the Blue Ghost Mission 4 lander (Firefly Aerospace, NASA’s CLPS Task Order CS-6), with a target landing date late 2029.

CLPS-LIMS is designed to analyse the element and isotope composition of lunar regolith dust in situ. Using laser ablation ionization and mass spectrometry, the instrument will characterize the geochemical makeup of the Moon’s surface material, supporting investigations into lunar resource potential and surface processes relevant to future exploration activities.

title: Initiative Afrique
intro_text:
African universities are important partners for the University of Bern. With the Initiative Afrique, the UniBE is placing a new emphasis on its internationalisation strategy, which brings together expertise in Africa and promotes networking. On this website, you will find information on existing activities and resources.

For many years, the University of Bern has been strongly committed to promoting science and fostering global research networks, particularly with partners in Africa. "We firmly believe that tackling global problems requires global cooperation – a principle on which our internationalisation strategy is based," says Hugues Abriel, Vice-Rector for Research and Innovation at the University of Bern.

The Initiative Afrique was created by the Vice-Rectorate Research and Innovation and the Vice-Rectorate International and Academic Careers. It serves as a platform for bundling all the research activities related to Africa and facilitating better networking within and outside the University of Bern.

title: School of Biomedical and Precision Engineering
intro_lead: Integrating Scientific Excellence and Precision Engineering for Future Technologies
intro_text:
The School of Biomedical and Precision Engineering SBPE is a newly established institution of the University of Bern and the Bern University of Applied Sciences promoting innovative technologies in both Precision Engineering and Biomedical Engineering. Precision Engineering is one of the critical technologies for successful innovations and provides access to future engineering demands while taking sustainability aspects into account. It combines disciplines like optics, microtechnology, physics, mechanical and electrical engineering, control and automation, modelling and simulations, mechatronics and robotics as well as material science, analytics and coatings.

At the core of the SBPE are the Master of Science in Precision Engineering and the Master of Science in Biomedical Engineering, academic collaborations between the University of Bern and the Bern University of Applied Sciences.

title: QuMat
intro_lead: Welcome to the W. Inäbnit Laboratory for Molecular Quantum Materials  (QuMat)
intro_text: The W. Inäbnit Laboratory for Molecular Quantum Materials (QuMat) is located at the Department of Chemistry, Biochemistry and Pharmaceutical Sciences (DCBP) of the University of Bern. In 2025, our research group, jointly with physics teams from the University of Basel, has established the new WSS Research Center for Molecular Quantum Systems (molQ). Our laboratory is devoted to the tailored design, synthesis and characterisation of novel organic molecules for molecular quantum materials, opening up exciting avenues in the pursuit of state-of-the-art topological quantum bits.

titlealt: Department of Social Anthropology and Cultural Studies
title: Department of Social Anthropology and Cultural Studies
intro_lead: Welcome to the Department of Social Anthropology and Cultural Studies (SACS)
intro_text:
The Department of Social Anthropology and Cultural Studies (SACS) is a new unit of the Faculty of Humanities that brings together the units of Social Anthropology, Religious Studies, Middle East Studies and Muslim Societies. 
Researchers at SACS analyse institutions, practices and norms of societies worldwide with a systematic-empirical orientation. 

Their research is organised in five research clusters. Within these clusters, academics from different disciplines and fields conduct joint and interdisciplinary research. They investigate current developments in a global and historical context and analyse the conditions of change and transformation from multiple perspectives. SACS makes an important contribution to the university's specialisations in intercultural knowledge, sustainability, politics, and administration. A transversal area shared by all three units strengthens methodological training and outreach. 

At the moment, the new department is being set up and therefore in a phase of transition. Students enrolled in previous programmes can obtain their degrees as planned.

title: Faculty of Theology
intro_lead: Study and research in Bern
intro_text:
At the Faculty of Theology of the University of Bern, students from diverse religious and ideological backgrounds have the opportunity to develop a well-founded academic understanding of the various aspects of Judaism, Christianity, and other religions through interdenominational and interreligious dialogue. Teaching and research at the Faculty focus on Old Catholic and Protestant theology, Jewish studies, and interreligious studies. A strong practical orientation is an integral part of the Faculty’s contemporary and critical profile.

On our website, you will find information about the Bachelor's and Master's programs in theology – offered as integrated studies, major, or minor – as well as details about the Faculty and its members
attr_dc_description:
At the Faculty of Theology of the University of Bern, students from diverse religious and ideological backgrounds form a scholarly opinion about aspects of Judaism, Christianity and other religions through dialogue with a range of confessions and religions. Teaching and research at Bern stands out with its strong emphasis on Old Catholic and Protestant Theology, Jewish Studies and Interreligious Studies. In addition, the modern and critical profile of our faculty supports strong practical training. 
On our website we offer information about BA and MA degrees in Old Catholic and Protestant Theology. They can be completed as an integrated programme of study or as a major or minor. Furthermore, we provide information about the Faculty itself and its members

Our website provides information about the bachelor and master’s degree programs in theology as a general studies program and as a major or minor course of study; it is also an introduction to the Faculty and its members.

title: Lifelong Learning Center
intro_lead: The ongoing pursuit of learning
intro_text:
The University of Bern believes that learning is a dynamic process that continues throughout every phase of life. With transfer-oriented offerings, the University makes it possible for several different target groups to engage in lifelong learning and gives them access to research-based knowledge.

The Lifelong Learning Center (LLC) is the service and competence center for lifelong learning at the University of Bern. It brings together the transfer-oriented offerings of continuing education, Children’s University, courses for high school students and Senior Citizens’ University under one roof and supports faculties, institutes and external partners with sound advice and tailor-made educational programs. As an interface between academia and practice, the LLC develops innovative learning formats, promotes interdisciplinary exchange, and strengthens the dialog between the University, business, and society.

title: Digital In/Justice Initiative
intro_text:
With the Digital In/Justice Initiative, we aim to create a transdisciplinary network that brings together scholars, activists, artists, and civil society organizations to critically engage with digital technologies, infrastructures and practices. Rooted in the University of Bern’s Digitalization Strategy 2030 and initiated by a project group funded by the digitalization commission of the UniBE, the initiative investigates how data and AI both reproduce and resist structural inequalities.

We build on intersectional and transdisciplinary approaches to establish a network to rethink digital futures through the lens of digital justice—a concept that foregrounds the systemic impacts of racism, sexism, ableism, and other forms of oppression embedded in digital technologies, infrastructures and practices. Our work aims not only to analyze injustices, but also to reimagine and shape more just, inclusive, and transformative digital worlds.
attr_dc_description: Initiative at the University of Bern exploring digital (in)justices through events, research, and a network for intersectional and critical AI studies.

titlealt: University of Bern and McGill Partnership
title: UniBe McGill Partnership
intro_lead: Advancing Precision Medicine Together
intro_text:
The University of Bern – McGill Partnership in Precision Medicine is a strategic collaboration between two leading institutions: the University of Bern (Switzerland) and McGill University (Montreal, Canada). 
The partnership is jointly funded by both institutions, reflecting their shared investment in advancing global health.
Officially launched in June 2024 through a Memorandum of Understanding, the partnership is built on a shared commitment to advancing precision health and medicine on a global scale. 

At its heart, the partnership addresses major global health challenges—especially the growing burden of metabolic diseases like diabetes and obesity—while also exploring other key therapeutic areas where precision approaches can make a transformative impact.
